rulings.Case Title: DANNY BLACK ET AL VS. ALLIS-CHALMERS CORPORATION PRODUCT LIABILITY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing the Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 29, 2019, line 1. DEFENDANT SLAKEY BROTHERS, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant Slakey Brothers, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Viewing all inferences in favor of Plaintiffs, Defendant Slakey Brothers Inc. failed to shift burden. (CCP § 437c(p)(2); Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 843, 850-51.) Mr. Black testified that he worked at the Rainbow Bakery facility located in Sacramento from 1986 to 2006. (Plaintiffs' Exhibit A, Deposition of Danny Black (Depo. Black) at p. 826:12-21; see Defendant's Exhibit 11.) Mr. Black's job duties included replacing gaskets and packing on pumps and valves. (Depo. Black at pp. 831:3-4, 831:14-832:1, 832:7-13, 823:14-20.) Mr. Black testified that the gaskets and packing were acquired or ordered from Slakey Brothers Inc. and recalls seeing Slakey Brothers Inc. trucks unload deliveries. (Depo. Black at pp. 1116:13-16, 1127:9-12.) Even if Slakey Brothers Inc. shifted burden, Plaintiffs provide evidence that raise triable issues of fact. (CCP § 437c(p)(2); Saelzer v. Advanced Group (2001) 25 Cal.4th 763, 780-781.) Slakey Brothers Inc. invoices demonstrate that Slakey Brothers Inc. in Sacramento delivered products to the Rainbow Bakery facility. (See Plaintiffs' Exhibit B.) The invoices, Mr. Black's deposition testimony, the Declaration of Mr. Black, and the Declaration of Charles Ay raise triable issues of fact as to Mr. Black's exposure to asbestos-containing products supplied by Defendant Slakey Brothers Inc. (See Plaintiffs' Exhibit C, Decl. of Danny Black; see Decl. Charles Ay at 20-21.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: DANNY BLACK ET AL VS. ALLIS-CHALMERS CORPORATION PRODUCT LIABILITY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION / Notice Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing the Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 29, 2019, line 1. DEFENDANT VALLEY PIPE & SUPPLY, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Defendant Valley Pipe & Supply, Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of Plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant Valley Pipe & Supply, Inc. failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that Plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain clear and convincing evidence that Defendant Valley Pipe & Supply, Inc. acted with the requisite malice, fraud, or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. (See Decl. Scott R. Herndon, Exhibit E, Exhibit F, Exhibit G, Exhibit H.) The burden of production "should not shift without stringent review of the direct, circumstantial and inferential evidence." (Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Construction Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64, 83.) Even if Plaintiffs' discovery response were factually devoid, Defendant Valley Pipe & Supply Inc. did not conduct comprehensive discovery as to punitive damages. Defendants cannot rely on a plaintiff's factually devoid discovery responses in the absence of conducting comprehensive discovery. (Id. at 83; see Andrews v. Foster Wheeler LLC (2006) 138 Cal.App.4th 96, 102-103.) If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of a reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: DANNY BLACK ET AL VS. ALLIS-CHALMERS CORPORATION PRODUCT LIABILITY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT - Alta Building Material Co.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing the Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 29, 2019, line 1. *** Part 1 of 2 *** Defendant Alta Building Material Co.'s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant Alta Building Material Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Taking all evidence and inferences in favor of Plaintiffs, Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that Plaintiffs do not possess, and cannot reasonable obtain evidence that Mr. Black was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials sold, supplied or distributed by Defendant Alta Building Material Co. and therefore there exists no evidence that Defendant caused Mr. Black's alleged injuries. Mr. Black testified that he cut and stapled CertainTeed asbestos-containing roof felt in the early 1960s and identified Defendant Alta Building Material Co.'s as the supplier of the felt. (Plaintiffs' Exhibit A, Deposition Black at pp. 59:8-20, 66:5-22, 67:3-8, 438:17-22, 448:14-18, 1655:20-1656:14.) CertainTeed offered "Perforated Saturated Asbestos Felt" from the 1950s until 1975, which was serviced by its roofing plant in Richmond, California and offered for sale in the geographic region. (Decl. Edward E. Hartley, Exhibit R.) Defendant Alta Building Material Co. failed to shift burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Even if Defendant Alta Building Material Co. sustained its initial burden, Mr. Black's deposition testimony, the Declaration of Mr. Black, the Declaration of Charles Ay, and the Deposition of Sam Siri create a triable issues of fact as to whether Mr. Black was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials sold, supplied or distributed by Defendant Alta Building Material Co. (Decl. of Danny Black; Decl. Charles Ay; see Plaintiffs' Exhibit A, Deposition of Sam Siri.) Defendant Alta Building Material Co.'s motion for summary adjudication of Plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant Alta Building Material Co. failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that Plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain clear and convincing evidence that Defendant Alta Building Material Co. acted with the requisite malice, fraud, or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. (See Decl. Edward E. Hartley, Exhibit C, Exhibit D, Exhibit E, Exhibit F.) The burden of production "should not shift without stringent review of the direct, circumstantial and inferential evidence." (Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Construction Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64, 83.) Even if Plaintiffs' discovery response were factually devoid, Defendant Alta Building Material Co. did not conduct comprehensive discovery as to punitive damages. Defendants cannot rely on a plaintiff's factually devoid discovery responses in the absence of conducting comprehensive discovery. (Id. at 83; see Andrews v. Foster Wheeler LLC (2006) 138 Cal.App.4th 96, 102-103.) *** End of Part 1 - Tentative Ruling continues in Part 2 of 2 ***

rulings.Case Title: ** TRANSFERRED TO SACRAMENTO COMPLEX LIT** JERRY SNYDER VS. DURO DYNE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing the Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, January 29, 2019, line 2. DEFENDANT CAMPBELL CONSTRUCTION CO.'S MOTION TO TRANSFER. Defendant Campbell Construction Co.'s motion to transfer venue is granted. No opposition. Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 397(c) and upon payment of all necessary fees, the case shall be transferred to Superior Court of California, County of Sacramento. The clerk of this court is authorized to transfer all the pleading and papers to the court clerk of Sacramento County. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of a reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30 p.m. stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/CML)
